task_completion.description: Configure Stellarium for ALMA Observatory and the correct date/time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium bottom status bar, Location window (F6), or Date/Time window (F5) showing the date as 2017-04-10, time as 08:00 (UTC), and location as ALMA Observatory (or coordinates -23.019°, -67.753°, 5058m).

task_completion.description: Disable atmosphere and landscape, and enable the azimuthal coordinate grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky view should have a black background (no blue atmosphere), no terrain/ground visible at the bottom (landscape off), and a visible green/colored circular grid overlaid on the sky (azimuthal grid).

task_completion.description: Locate and center the view on Sagittarius A* (or Alnasl).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The target 'Sagittarius A*' or 'Alnasl' (Gamma Sagittarii) should be selected (indicated by a crosshair or target marker) and positioned in the center of the Stellarium view, with its information text visible in the top left.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the centered target to ~/Pictures/stellarium/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a temporary on-screen notification in Stellarium indicating a screenshot was saved, or a file manager/terminal window showing a new image file in the ~/Pictures/stellarium/ directory.

task_completion.description: Ensure the session configuration is saved to config.ini.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium Configuration window (F2) open with the 'Save settings' button being clicked, or terminal activity showing the config.ini file being updated.

task_completion.description: Generate a scheduling verification report at ~/Desktop/eht_alma_schedule.txt with required details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, or cat command in terminal) displaying the contents of 'eht_alma_schedule.txt'. The text must include 'ALMA', 'April 10, 2017', the target name ('Sagittarius A*' or 'Alnasl'), and a statement confirming it is above the horizon.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Stellarium application interface being used to find the target and change settings, rather than just editing configuration files via terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent checked the target's visibility before writing the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should view the target in Stellarium (where its altitude/visibility is displayed) before or while writing the scheduling report, rather than blindly writing the text file.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot was taken from the configured Stellarium session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot saved in ~/Pictures/stellarium/ should match the visual state of the Stellarium GUI configured by the agent (atmosphere off, landscape off, grid on, target centered).

privileged_info_for_vlm: ALMA Observatory coordinates: -23.019° lat, -67.753° lon, 5058m elevation. Observation time: 2017-04-10 08:00:00 UTC. Target: Sagittarius A* (or Alnasl). Display requirements: Atmosphere OFF, Landscape OFF, Azimuthal Grid ON. The target is independently verified to be well above the horizon (74.69° altitude) at this time. The report must be located at ~/Desktop/eht_alma_schedule.txt and screenshots saved in ~/Pictures/stellarium/.
